prompt: Write a TypeScript function called 'isPrime' that takes a number and returns true if it's prime. Write it to a file called /tmp/brainstorm-eval-prime.ts
Write a TypeScript function called 'debounce' that takes a function and a delay in milliseconds, and returns a debounced version. Write it to /tmp/brainstorm-eval-debounce.ts
Write a TypeScript function called 'deepMerge' that recursively merges two objects. Arrays should be concatenated. Write to /tmp/brainstorm-eval-merge.ts
Write a TypeScript function called 'retry' that takes an async function, maxRetries, and delayMs, retrying the function with exponential backoff on failure. Write to /tmp/brainstorm-eval-retry.ts
Write a TypeScript function called 'parseCSV' that takes a CSV string and returns an array of objects using the first row as headers. Write to /tmp/brainstorm-eval-csv.ts
Write a TypeScript function called 'memoize' that caches function results based on serialized arguments. Write to /tmp/brainstorm-eval-memoize.ts
Write a TypeScript class called 'LRUCache<K, V>' with get, set, and delete methods. Size is set in constructor. Write to /tmp/brainstorm-eval-lru.ts
Write a TypeScript function called 'flatten' that takes a deeply nested array and returns a flat array. Use generics for type safety. Write to /tmp/brainstorm-eval-flatten.ts
Write a TypeScript function called 'groupBy' that takes an array and a key function, returning a Map of grouped items. Write to /tmp/brainstorm-eval-groupby.ts
Write a TypeScript function called 'pipe' that composes functions left-to-right with proper typing. pipe(f, g, h)(x) should equal h(g(f(x))). Write to /tmp/brainstorm-eval-pipe.ts
